company_events.description: Management guided Q1 FY2027 revenue to about $78.0B ±2% and noted the outlook assumes no Data Center compute revenue from China—increasing transparency on geopolitical revenue risk.
Blackwell-generation GPUs and related systems continue to drive a platform upgrade cycle across hyperscalers and enterprise AI clusters.
CUDA, networking (e.g., InfiniBand/Ethernet), and software bundles deepen switching costs for many AI training and inference footprints.
Custom ASIC programs at large customers and AMD’s MI-series roadmap raise long-run pricing and share pressure—even if near-term demand remains supply-constrained.

narrative_summary: The FY2026 print is dominated by record Data Center revenue and continued scarcity economics for leading-edge AI accelerators. Forward debate centers on how much of the next year is already embedded in backlog/capex plans, the China policy path, and whether gross margins re-expand as mix and yields improve. Partners (foundry/assembly) and power/data-center real estate remain the practical bottlenecks—not lack of demand, in most scenarios.
